_comment: Deterministic golden set for the offline retrieval gate (recall@k / MRR over BM25 + RRF). Self-contained chunks (no chunker dependency). Expand with real corpus/queries over time. End-to-end answer quality is graded separately by scripts/run_eval.py.

corpus.text: Retrieval-augmented generation combines a retriever with a language model to ground answers in external documents.
Hybrid retrieval fuses dense vector search with sparse BM25 keyword matching using reciprocal rank fusion.
A cross-encoder reranker rescores candidate passages by jointly encoding the query and each passage together.
Prompt caching stores a stable prefix so repeated requests reuse it, reducing token cost and latency.
The minimum cacheable prefix is a few thousand tokens; shorter prompts silently fail to cache.
Celery workers process background tasks asynchronously using Redis as the broker and result backend.
Idempotent task handling with acks_late prevents a redelivered message from running the pipeline twice.
RAGAS measures faithfulness, answer relevancy, and context precision for retrieval-augmented systems.

queries.query: how does hybrid retrieval fuse dense and sparse
what does a cross-encoder reranker do
how does prompt caching reduce token cost
ragas faithfulness answer relevancy context precision
reciprocal rank fusion bm25 vector search
